channel.title: Sreayan De
channel.description: Writing about systems programming, distributed systems, and things I'm building.

item.title: building a tokenizer cache from scratch
item.description: a dict, a linked list, and a lock walk into an LLM pipeline. the result is a 10x speedup on repeated tokenization.

item.title: flashattention from first principles
item.description: i knew how attention worked in C++ but couldn't read the FlashAttention paper. the missing piece was one sentence: a sentence is a matrix. once that clicked, i wrote both kernels in CUDA and watched the tiled version pull 3x on a T4.

item.title: attention is all you need, in raw c++
item.description: i implemented a transformer encoder from scratch in c++ with zero dependencies. matrix math, multi-head attention, SIMD matmul, 4-bit quantization, and a full forward pass you can hold in one file.

item.title: building a distributed rate-limited http server in go
item.description: i built a go http server with distributed rate limiting via redis and lua, context timeouts, prometheus metrics, graceful shutdown, and load tested it at ~7,600 rps.

item.title: redis token bucket rate limiting in go
item.description: i needed a real per-ip limiter across multiple go instances, so i pushed the bucket into redis and made refill + consume atomic with lua.

item.title: the cache is lying to you
item.description: why the most common performance optimization in distributed systems is also one of its most dangerous failure modes

item.title: understanding llm inference engineering: why your laptop can't keep up
item.description: i benchmarked a small llm on my cpu and finally saw where inference time actually goes.

item.title: how llms actually run at scale
item.description: a systems deep dive into what it takes to serve a 405B parameter model to thousands of users.

item.title: implementing mapreduce in go
item.description: building a concurrent mapreduce engine from scratch to count discord messages.

item.title: building mini-git
item.description: i'm building git from scratch in C++.

item.title: how i accidentally derived mapreduce
item.description: i tried to count discord messages and ended up reinventing a 2004 google paper.

item.title: hello, world
item.description: first post. why i'm writing, what to expect, and why twitter wasn't enough.
